rationale_en: Resets duty rates for only 40 specific tariff lines, a real but narrow price change with no expected visible effect on aggregate customs revenue or import volumes.
Governs NPL collection procedure for banks, a real credit-quality lever but administrative in nature, so only a partial expected effect on aggregate private-credit measures.
Targeted subsidized housing facilities for low-income households in small towns is a real but modest-scale program relative to the nationwide housing stock it is scored against.
An enforcement bylaw against smuggling penalties is administrative and would not be expected to move the level of the exchange rate.
Anti-smuggling enforcement bears only indirectly on the accuracy of recorded trade statistics, with no expected effect on measured trade volumes.
A national CNG-station buildout is a real fuel-switching instrument with a genuine but partial expected effect on the gasoline-versus-gas consumption split.
A single fiscal-year subsidized-loan employment facility is a real but marginal instrument against national unemployment and aggregate private credit.
A narrow court ruling on fertilizer pricing methodology has no expected visible effect on aggregate subsidy and transfer spending.
A ruling on a single disputed grain shipment's customs classification has no expected effect on aggregate customs duties or cereal trade.
Voids a valuation rule for used machinery only, too narrow a segment to expect any visible effect on aggregate customs duties.
Establishing the corporate charter of a single state farm company is administrative and negligible against national agricultural output.
TPS-OIC tariff preferences are a real trade-policy instrument, but the agreement's slow, partial implementation across members limits its expected effect on Iran's exports.
A single year's annual budget, even a wartime one, is one force among many against the scale of national GDP, matching the rubric's budget-versus-GDP anchor.
The annual budget law is the direct legal instrument that fixes government expenditure, so it structurally defines this series.
Deficit financing in the budget fed into monetary expansion, a real but partial and indirect channel to inflation alongside wartime and oil-revenue shocks.
A single province's wheat working-group ruling is narrow administrative machinery with no expected effect on the national guaranteed wheat price.
A ruling on allocation procedure for one land-use case is routine administrative machinery, not a driver of the aggregate agricultural land stock.
Annulling one circular on fallow-land seizure is a narrow procedural correction with no expected effect on the recorded agricultural land stock.
A court review of the national housing-supply bylaw touches a real housing-policy instrument, but a single ruling on one clause has only a partial expected effect.
A tariff ruling on the exact regulated service being measured is a real price channel, but subscription counts are dominated by secular mobile substitution, limiting expected effect.
A ruling on one 1984 call-rate increase provision is a narrow historical validation with no expected effect on subscription counts or budget totals.
Clarifies which multi-tier FX rate applies to one import-financing channel, a real but narrow procedural piece of the broader sanctions-era FX system.
The justification itself is a weak, tangential stretch from a tax-circular ruling to the exchange rate, with no plausible channel.
A narrow withholding-tax ruling on foreign lender interest has no expected direct effect on the exchange rate level.
A targeted insurance-premium exemption for micro-employers is a real but narrow labor-cost instrument, partial against aggregate employment-share and pension-flow series.
A narrow tax-administration procedural determination that the justification itself says does not alter policy or rates.
Expands the taxable base for FX-asset holders in a specific circumstance, a real but narrow clarification, partial against aggregate income-tax revenue.
A narrow procedural annulment of one tax circular's retroactive application, with no policy or rate effect.
Another narrow tax-circular annulment, purely a procedural matter within tax administration.
A tax exemption on one commodity is a real, direct tax-revenue instrument, but too narrow to expect a visible effect on aggregate goods-and-services tax revenue.
A price effect on one commodity is one of many small drivers of food consumption, with no expected visible effect on the sugar consumption series.

Amends one article of the subsidy-targeting bylaw governing cash transfers and procurement pricing, a real but parametric adjustment within the existing subsidy system rather than a fresh structural reform.
A single-article amendment to a subsidy bylaw is too narrow a parametric change to expect any visible effect on national GDP or CPI, unlike the founding subsidy-reform law.
Credit facilities to expand one refinery's capacity are a real but plant-specific investment, partial against national refined-product output.
Refining capacity at one plant has no plausible channel to oil rents, which are driven by resource extraction, not downstream refining.
Protecting the wartime rationing system from black-market erosion is a real but protective/enforcement measure, one force among several shaping prices and subsidy costs.
A technical bond-conversion mechanism for land-reform receivables is a financing detail with no plausible direct channel to national GDP.
Resolving a financing bottleneck in land-reform payments is a real but partial and indirect contributor to completing the land-transfer process.
Converting one program's receivables into bonds is a minor, early-stage instrument, negligible against the aggregate national liabilities series.
A deadline extension for farm-debt repayment is a marginal relief measure, not expected to move aggregate agricultural value added.
A procedural paperwork mechanism for completing land-reform sales has no plausible direct effect on agricultural value added.
Raising state agricultural-credit appropriations is a real financing instrument, but modest in scale and one of several forces on agricultural value added.

Creates Iran's participation-bond (sukuk) instrument, a genuinely foundational new capital-markets asset class, though its aggregate market impact built gradually rather than as a single structural break.
A cargo-handling fee on exports is a narrow, marginal trade cost with no expected visible effect on export volumes or values.
A first-phase, pilot-scale share transfer to workers is a real but early and partial precursor to full privatization, not itself a structural change to industrial value added.
Establishes the institutional enforcement body for price controls, a real but indirect channel since the bylaw itself does not set prices.
A general revision of trade-licensing rules just after the Iran-Iraq War is a real trade-policy channel, but one of many such bylaw amendments over time.
A procedural fix to duty collection specific to free trade zones is too narrow to move national customs or trade aggregates.
A budget-line top-up for one consumer-protection fund is a narrow appropriation, negligible against aggregate subsidy and transfer spending.
Setting airport landing fees is a price-schedule matter with no plausible channel to the number of air passengers carried.
Penalizing violators of an existing transport tariff is an enforcement measure, not expected to move recorded freight or passenger volumes.
A sector-specific livestock regulation is a real but partial instrument among several shaping livestock production.
A tariff-rate reset for a specific goods list is a real but narrow instrument, partial against aggregate import and trade-tax series.
A broad, across-the-board cut in commercial-profit rates is a real trade-liberalizing instrument with a meaningful but partial expected effect alongside other liberalization steps.
Adding a single duty item for free trade zones is a narrow administrative change with negligible effect on aggregate customs revenue.
A minor textual amendment to one bylaw article has no expected visible effect on merchandise trade flows.
A narrow addendum to FX facility agency-contract terms has no plausible channel to the exchange rate level.
A minor adjustment to FX-loan agency-contract mechanics is one small input into aggregate private credit, with no expected visible effect.
A single administered telephone tariff is one of many small CPI-basket components, with no expected visible effect on the index.
Blocking one planned electricity-tariff increase is a real administered-price channel, but one component among many in the CPI and a price-inelastic driver of consumption.
A ruling on the price-control oversight body's authority is a real but indirect institutional channel into consumer and wholesale prices.
Reviewing an amended clause of the Direct Taxes Act is a real tax-liability channel, but a single-clause ruling has only a partial expected effect on aggregate tax revenue.
A ruling on tax-assessment administrative procedure is a narrow machinery matter, not a change to tax policy or rates.
A ruling on one year's minimum-wage decree touches wage-price dynamics, but as a single-year procedural review its expected effect on CPI is partial.
The minimum wage is a real binding floor for wage earners, but a court ruling on one year's decree has only a partial expected effect on poverty and wage-share measures.
The telecom company was one of the largest single Article 44 privatizations, and its listing was large enough to expect a material, visible effect on market capitalization.
A change in ownership of the telecom operator has no expected effect on the number of fixed-line subscriptions, an operational metric it does not touch.
Annulling one Ministry of Commerce trade directive is a narrow, single-instrument correction, negligible against aggregate trade and customs series.
Setting ETF trading commissions is a micro-level fee parameter, negligible against aggregate stock trading value.
A small compact-fluorescent-lamp subsidy program amendment is negligible against national per-capita electricity and energy consumption.
A decree concerning the national grid operator is a real institutional link, but without specifics on capacity investment its expected effect is partial.
Opening the stock and OTC markets to foreign investors is a real structural liberalization, though actual foreign participation stayed limited under sanctions, capping its expected effect.
The justification itself notes portfolio investment is not FDI, so this stock-market rule has no genuine channel to FDI inflows.
A fuel-switching bylaw for public transport is a real demand-management instrument, but one component among several fuel-consumption policies of the era.
A narrow technical addition to one bylaw clause governing import licensing procedure, with no expected visible effect on import levels or tariffs.
A broader revision of trade-licensing and tariff operating rules is a real channel, but its implementation-level nature gives it only a partial expected effect.
Part of the broader post-war trade-liberalization wave, a real but partial contributor among several such bylaw amendments.
A generic, undetailed decree updating trade-bylaw operating rules, with no specific substance suggesting a visible effect on trade flows.
A narrow customs-administration note specific to free trade zones, negligible against national merchandise trade aggregates.
Adding one exemption item to a duty-exempt list is a narrow, negligible change against aggregate import tariffs.

Formally opening exchanges and OTC markets to foreign capital is a structural liberalization of the whole capital-markets domain, though actual sanctions-constrained participation limits its realized reach.
Creating a regulated housing-investment fund vehicle is a real housing-finance channel, but a niche instrument modest relative to the overall housing stock.
A court ruling narrowing part of an industrial-modernization support scheme is a narrow, partial annulment with no expected visible effect on industrial value added.

Removing a relocation interest-subsidy is a real, targeted channel to the specific provincial industrial-distribution series it is scored against, but one incentive among several decentralization tools.
Fixing the start date of an existing tax exemption is a real but narrow timing clarification, not the exemption policy itself, so only a partial effect on tax revenue.
Article 44 privatization bylaws reassigned ownership of a large share of the state economy, a major structural instrument we would expect to leave a visible mark on national output and investment.
Article 44 was implemented largely through listing former state enterprises on the exchange, a major direct driver of market capitalization and trading growth through the 2000s-2010s.
A large share of divested assets were industrial firms, a real but gradual restructuring channel, partial against aggregate industry and manufacturing value added.
Refining the installment-payment mechanism for privatization-share buyers is a real operational lever affecting the pace of divestiture, but a narrow parametric piece of the broader program.
A narrow, distributive share-transfer channel to war veterans and workers, negligible against the aggregate capital-markets domain.
A licensing-conformity requirement for existing mine concessions is administrative housekeeping with no expected visible effect on mineral output.
Governs licensing/exploitation terms for mines, a real regulatory channel to mine production value, but one narrow provision among the sector's rules.
A small technical remark added to one tariff line is negligible against aggregate merchandise trade.
A textual amendment affecting two specific tariff lines is a narrow customs-classification change, negligible against aggregate trade.
Annulling specific provisions of the trade bylaw during sanctions is narrow, with no expected visible effect on aggregate tariff rates or trade flows.
A government service-fee schedule is a real, direct non-tax revenue instrument, but fee income is typically modest relative to total government revenue.
A generic public financial-management reform bylaw, as the justification itself notes, with no specific instrument that would move budget totals.
A narrow information-transmission procedure for an existing debt instrument, not a change to debt policy itself, negligible against aggregate government liabilities.
A judicial clarification of landlord-tenant relations during the land-reform era is a real but interpretive/legal channel, not itself a land-transfer instrument.
A single legal clarification of landlord-tenant obligations is an indirect labor-relations channel, negligible against aggregate agricultural value added.
